class.name: SQLiteDatabase
class.brief_description: A SQLite database resource. Can be either in-memory or load a database from a file.
class.description: A SQLite database resource that provides high-level access to a SQLite database. It supports both in-memory databases and persistent databases stored in files. This class offers methods for executing queries, managing tables, transactions, indexes, user-defined functions, and more.

method.name: attach_database
method.description: Attaches an additional database file to the current connection using the given [param alias]. Returns [code]true[/code] on success.

method.name: backup_async
method.description: Starts an asynchronous backup of the current database to the specified file [param path]. Returns a [SQLiteBackup] object that can be used to monitor progress.

method.name: backup_to
method.description: Performs a synchronous backup of the current database to the specified file [param path]. Returns [code]true[/code] on success.

method.name: begin_transaction
method.description: Starts a new database transaction.

method.name: close
method.description: Closes the database connection.

method.name: commit_transaction
method.description: Commits the current database transaction.

method.name: create_aggregate
method.description: Registers a new aggregate (window) function with the database. The [param step_callable] is called for each row, and [param final_callable] is called once at the end to compute the final result.

method.name: create_collation
method.description: Registers a custom collation (sorting) function with the given name. The [param callable] should take two [String] arguments and return an [int] (-1, 0, or 1).

method.name: create_function
method.description: Registers a new scalar SQL function with the database. The callable is invoked whenever the function is used in a query.

method.name: create_index
method.description: Creates an index on a table.

method.name: create_query
method.description: Creates a new query object.

method.name: create_savepoint
method.description: Creates a named savepoint within the current transaction. Savepoints allow partial rollbacks.

method.name: create_table
method.description: Creates a new table.

method.name: delete_rows
method.description: Deletes rows from a table.

method.name: deserialize_object
method.description: Deserializes data from the database into the provided [param object] using a [param key]. Returns [code]true[/code] on success.

method.name: detach_database
method.description: Detaches a previously attached database by its [param alias]. Returns [code]true[/code] on success.

method.name: drop_index
method.description: Drops an index.

method.name: drop_table
method.description: Drops a table.

method.name: enable_load_extension
method.description: Enables or disables the ability to load SQLite extensions.

method.name: execute_query
method.description: Create and execute a query directly.

method.name: execute_script
method.description: Executes all SQL statements from a script file at the given [param path]. Returns [code]true[/code] if all statements executed successfully.

method.name: export_to_json
method.description: Exports the entire content of the specified table as a JSON string.

method.name: get_changes
method.description: Returns the number of rows that were changed, inserted, or deleted by the most recently completed INSERT, UPDATE, or DELETE statement.

method.name: get_columns
method.description: Returns the columns of a table.

method.name: get_database_filename
method.description: Returns the filename of the specified database (default is "main"). Useful when working with attached databases.

method.name: get_db_config
method.description: Retrieves the current value of a database configuration option.

method.name: get_db_status
method.description: Retrieves runtime status information from the database connection.

method.name: get_global_status
method.description: Returns the current value of a global SQLite status counter identified by [param op]. These counters provide information about the overall SQLite library usage and performance. If [param reset] is [code]true[/code], the selected counter is reset to zero after its value is retrieved.

method.name: get_last_insert_rowid
method.description: Returns the row ID of the most recent successful [code]INSERT[/code] operation performed on the database connection.

method.name: get_limit
method.description: Returns the current value of a runtime limit identified by [param id].

method.name: get_sqlite
method.description: Returns the underlying [SQLite] object.

method.name: get_total_changes
method.description: Returns the total number of rows that have been modified, inserted, or deleted since the database connection was opened.

method.name: import_from_json
method.description: Imports data into the table specified by [param table_name] from a JSON string provided in [param json_string]. The JSON data must be structured as an array of objects where each object represents a row. Returns [code]true[/code] if the import succeeds, otherwise [code]false[/code].

method.name: insert_row
method.description: Inserts a single row into the table specified by [param table_name], using the key-value pairs in [param value] where each key corresponds to a column name and each value is the data to insert. Returns a [SQLiteQuery] representing the executed or prepared insert operation.

method.name: insert_rows
method.description: Inserts multiple rows into the table specified by [param table_name]. Each element in [param values] is a dictionary representing a row, where keys correspond to column names. Returns a [SQLiteQuery] representing the batch insert operation.

method.name: instantiate_object
method.description: Creates and returns an object instance from the table specified by [param table_name], using [param key] as the primary identifier. The returned value depends on the stored data and serialization format.

method.name: interrupt
method.description: Interrupts any currently executing database operation on this connection. This can be used to safely abort long-running queries.

method.name: is_autocommit
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the database is currently in autocommit mode, meaning there is no active transaction.

method.name: is_readonly
method.description: Returns [code]true[/code] if the database specified by [param db_name] is read-only, otherwise returns [code]false[/code].

method.name: load_from
method.description: Loads a database from the file at the given [param path]. Returns [code]true[/code] if successful.

method.name: open_blob
method.description: Opens a BLOB handle for incremental I/O on the specified column. The [param db_name], [param table_name], and [param column_name] identify the BLOB, while [param rowid] selects the row. If [param read_write] is [code]true[/code], the BLOB is opened for reading and writing; otherwise it is read-only.

method.name: release_memory
method.description: Attempts to free up to [param bytes] of memory from SQLite's internal caches. Returns the number of bytes actually released.

method.name: release_savepoint
method.description: Releases the savepoint identified by [param name], committing any changes made since the savepoint was created while keeping the transaction active.

method.name: restore_async
method.description: Starts an asynchronous restore operation from the database file at [param path]. Returns a [SQLiteBackup] object that can be used to monitor progress.

method.name: restore_from
method.description: Restores the database from the file at [param path], replacing the current contents. Returns [code]true[/code] if successful.

method.name: rollback_to_savepoint
method.description: Rolls back the database state to the savepoint identified by [param name], undoing any changes made after it was created.

method.name: rollback_transaction
method.description: Rolls back the current active database transaction, undoing all changes made since the transaction began. This restores the database to its previous committed state. Returns a [SQLiteQuery] representing the rollback operation.

method.name: select_rows
method.description:
Selects rows from the table specified by [param table_name]. The optional [param condition] defines the SQL [code]WHERE[/code] clause used to filter results.
If [param condition] is empty, all rows from the table are returned.
Returns a [SQLiteQuery] representing the executed or prepared [code]SELECT[/code] statement.

method.name: serialize_object
method.description: Serializes the given [param object] and stores it in the table specified by [param table_name] under the identifier [param key]. Returns [code]true[/code] if the operation succeeds.

method.name: set_authorizer
method.description: Sets an authorization callback defined by [param callable], which is invoked by SQLite to approve or deny certain operations during query execution.

method.name: set_busy_timeout
method.description: Sets the busy timeout in milliseconds to [param ms]. This determines how long SQLite will wait for a locked database before returning an error.

method.name: set_data
method.description: Sets the raw binary contents of the database using [param data]. This replaces the current database state with the provided serialized database data. It is typically used when loading a database from memory or transferring a database snapshot.

method.name: set_db_config
method.description: Configures a database option identified by [param op] with the value [param val]. Returns [code]true[/code] if the configuration succeeds.

method.name: set_foreign_keys_enabled
method.description: Enables or disables foreign key constraint enforcement based on [param enabled].

method.name: set_limit
method.description: Sets a runtime limit identified by [param id] to [param new_val] and returns the previous value of that limit.

method.name: set_progress_handler
method.description: Sets a progress handler that is invoked periodically during query execution after the specified number of virtual machine instructions defined by [param instructions].

method.name: set_resource
method.description: Associates the database with a resource located at [param path], allowing it to be managed or accessed through the engine's resource system.

method.name: set_soft_heap_limit
method.description: Sets a soft limit on the amount of heap memory SQLite is allowed to use, specified in bytes by [param bytes].

method.name: set_trace
method.description: Enables or disables SQL statement tracing based on [param enabled]. When enabled, executed statements can be monitored for debugging or logging purposes.

method.name: update_rows
method.description: Updates rows in the table specified by [param table_name] that match the SQL [code]WHERE[/code] clause provided in [param condition]. The [param value] dictionary defines the columns to update, where each key is a column name and each value is the new value to assign. Returns a [SQLiteQuery] representing the executed or prepared [code]UPDATE[/code] statement.

method.name: vacuum
method.description: Rebuilds the database file, reclaiming unused space and defragmenting it. Returns [code]true[/code] if the operation succeeds.

method.name: wal_checkpoint
method.description: Performs a checkpoint on the Write-Ahead Log (WAL) for the database specified by [param db_name], transferring changes into the main database file. Returns [code]true[/code] if the checkpoint operation succeeds.

members.member: Returns a dictionary containing information about the tables in the database. The dictionary keys are table names, and the values contain metadata describing each table, such as its columns and schema details.

signal.name: query_profiled
signal.description: Emitted after a query has finished executing with profiling enabled. Provides the executed SQL query as [param query_string] and the total time spent executing it in nanoseconds via[param execution_time_ns].

signal.name: query_progress
signal.description: Emitted periodically during the execution of a long-running query. Can be used to monitor execution or interrupt the query if needed.

signal.name: row_updated
signal.description: Emitted whenever a row is inserted, updated, or deleted. The type of operation is specified by [param update_operation], while [param db_name] and [param table_name] identify the affected database and table. The [param rowid] indicates the row that was modified.

signal.name: transaction_committed
signal.description: Emitted when the current transaction is successfully committed.

signal.name: transaction_rolled_back
signal.description: Emitted when the current transaction is rolled back and all changes are reverted.

signal.name: wal_updated
signal.description: Emitted when the Write-Ahead Log (WAL) is updated. The [param db_name] specifies the database, and [param pages_in_wal] indicates the number of pages currently stored in the WAL.
